headline: Agent infrastructure dominated the quarter, with persistent memory, tool integration, and long-running workflow patterns moving together.
body: Tool integration led the rotation (ratio 75), tracking a wave of agent tooling: Hermes added persistent memory and a skill library across sessions, and Agent-Reach extended agents to live internet context across Western and Chinese platforms. Long-running agent architecture came into focus with event-driven dormancy, durable checkpointing, and separated evaluation, alongside enterprise-managed MCP authentication and Antigravity tactics for large codebases. On the privacy front, privacy-preserving inference (ratio 92) and encrypted reasoning broke higher even as both technique families lagged on momentum. New entrants including distillation, robot policy, and several theory items (sign rank, Z2-index, list replicability) appeared without yet anchoring to coverage.
